pages.raw_text:
L. p. Hornberger, who managed
Governor Mead's campaign during
the late primaries, will give all of
his time during the present year to
the management of the Washing
ton exhibit at the A. Y. P. fair.
When Governor Mead was last
here, it is understood that Horn
berger cohld at that time have Sc
cured the position of executive
commissioner Of the Washington
exhibit, made vacant by the death
of Henry l.. Strobridge.


Mr.. Hornberger said he did not
want the place, but 1t Is understood
that he has since agreed to take it
and remain with the work until
after the fair is over. The place
pays about $500 per month and ex-
penses and was looked upon as a
very desirable berth. The other
members Of the state commission
seemed to think that Hornberger
was the logical candidate for the
place, and he was urged to accept
it, but would not listen to the sug-
gestion at the time. 1t is under
stood that he has since reconsider
ed the matter and concluded to go
ahead with the work. The state
appropriation for the exposition
was 100000.
